----- Original Message ----
From: Gmax gmax1@mindspring.com
To: JE jeeproject@yahoo.com
Sent: Friday, February 2, 2007 12:33:03 PM
Subject: Fwd: Bauble

Hi G,

I would not have asked you, but Isabel thought it
might be a good idea to run this by you, as I am
disposing of something that is for the extremely
wealthy. I am in the midst of raising some immediate
significant cash for our business.

Naturally, this needs to be worth your while, and I
will offer a 100 - 150K commission if you can place
this.

The item is made for a king or prince in 1610 and the
only other example is found in the Kremlin museum in
Moscow. It is a chariot automaton clock, which rolls
across the table and 12 automata do various things. On
the hour (and you should like this), the figure raises
his right arm for drinking, rolls his eyes and sticks
out its tongue. While the chariot rolls across the
table, the coach driver lifts his trident, the
elephants nod and the musician bears turn round on
themselves controlling the levers on the left side.

This is one of the most complex automaton engineering
feats of this area, and it still works! Although it is
very noisy when going!

This is a crown piece showpiece for those who have
everything and want something fantastic and KINGLY
that they can show off.

It's pretty large: height 53.3 cm (21 inches); Length
46 cm (18 inches), width 23.5 cm (9/25 inches).

As there has not been something like this for sale,
and the value is approximately 3 - 3.5 million. I want
1.4 and would suggest, if you are interested to offer
at 1.65 and take anything above 1.4. It has never been
through the rooms.

It's pretty cool. I know it is not for Ted, but we
thought you might know someone fabulously wealthy who
could afford it and think it was cool. This sort of
thing does appeal to tech sort of people, like myself.

I have numerous high quality pictures (external and
internal) and can give a condition report (it is
excellent for the period with only minor repairs). I
enclose just one.

I hope I am not offending you by asking you, but I
hope the considerabl six figure commission would make
it worth your while to consider. It just means finding
the right home.

Love,
me

feats of this area, and it still works! Although it is
